title: MAKING A DOCTOR’S APPOINTMENT
conversation: Grace is sick. She calls her doctor's office to make an appointment for today. Laura: Primary Medical Group. How may I help you? Grace: I'd like to make an appointment with Dr. Feinberg. Laura: Your name? Grace: Grace Lee Laura: What's the nature of your visit? Grace: I think I've come down with the flu. Laura: We have an opening tomorrow at three. Grace: I can't wait that long. I'm really sick! Laura: Well, unfortunately, Dr. Feinberg is booked solid today. If he were free at all, I would squeeze you in. Grace: Can anybody else see me? Laura: Hang on. Let me check . . . You're in luck. Dr. Wilson can see you at four o'clock this afternoon. Grace: Don't you have anything earlier today? Laura: No, I'm afraid not. We're short-staffed this week. Grace: So there's no way I can come in before four today? Laura: I'd like to help you, but my hands are tied. If I were you, I would grab this four o'clock appointment. Grace: Okay. I'll take it.

idioms.meaning_en: A formal way to ask about the reason or purpose of a visit or inquiry.
idioms.meaning_vi: Lý do hay mục đích của (buổi khám/cuộc gặp) là gì.
idioms.example: What's the nature of your business with the director?
idioms.context: What's the nature of your visit?

idioms.meaning_en: To begin to catch or suffer from an illness (usually a minor one).
idioms.example: I think I'm coming down with a cold because my throat is sore.
idioms.context: I think I've come down with the flu.

idioms.meaning_en: To have an available time slot in a schedule.
idioms.meaning_vi: Có lịch trống, có chỗ trống.
idioms.example: Does the dentist have an opening for a cleaning next Tuesday?
idioms.context: We have an opening tomorrow at three.

idioms.meaning_en: Completely full; having no available appointments or space.
idioms.meaning_vi: Đã kín lịch, không còn chỗ trống.
idioms.example: The hotel is booked solid for the holiday weekend.
idioms.context: Dr. Feinberg is booked solid today.

idioms.meaning_en: In any way or to any degree (used for emphasis).
idioms.example: I don't mind at all if you stay a bit longer.
idioms.context: If he were free at all, I would squeeze you in.

idioms.meaning_en: To manage to find time to see or help someone despite a busy schedule.
idioms.meaning_vi: Cố gắng sắp xếp thời gian để tiếp ai đó.
idioms.example: The manager is very busy, but she can squeeze you in for ten minutes.

idioms.meaning_en: To wait for a short period of time.
idioms.example: Hang on a second, I forgot my wallet upstairs.
You're in luck! We just had one cancellation for tonight.

idioms.meaning_en: A polite way of saying no or giving disappointing news.
idioms.meaning_vi: Tôi e là không (cách từ chối lịch sự).
idioms.example: Is there any dessert left? I'm afraid not.

idioms.meaning_en: Not having enough workers or employees to operate efficiently.
idioms.meaning_vi: Thiếu nhân sự, thiếu nhân viên.
idioms.example: Service is slow today because the restaurant is short-staffed.

idioms.meaning_en: Unable to act or help due to rules, regulations, or circumstances beyond control.
idioms.meaning_vi: Bó tay, không thể làm khác được do quy định.
idioms.example: I'd like to give you a refund, but my hands are tied by the store's policy.
idioms.context: I'd like to help you, but my hands are tied.
